title: Synthetic Splunk SOC detection review for browser-cache payload extraction
scenario: An AI assistant drafts a Splunk/SOC-style detection idea and release note for browser-cache / ClickFix-style payload extraction review.

detection_artifact.name: Browser cache extraction followed by script interpreter staging
detection_artifact.description: Synthetic review object for suspicious browser cache file access followed by script interpreter staging. The object is a detection-review fixture, not deployable detection logic.
detection_artifact.risk_hypothesis: A user interaction lure may lead to cached payload extraction and local interpreter staging.
detection_artifact.splunk_search_pseudocode: index=synthetic_endpoint sourcetype=synthetic_process (parent_process_name IN (browser.exe, synthetic_browser.exe)) AND (process_name IN (powershell.exe, cmd.exe, wscript.exe)) AND file_path="*\\Browser\\Cache\\*"
known_limitations: No live endpoint telemetry is included.
No preserved alert evidence is included.
Pseudocode is not a production deployment artifact.

safe_claim: This artifact demonstrates Hoxline's controlled validation and claim-boundary workflow for an AI-assisted security draft.
